free_text: Study of proton induced nuclear reactions on
molybdenum: cross section measurements and
theoretical calculations
Zagazig University
High purity commercial metal 20 um Mo foils and Cu
monitor foils.
The 2 stacks
containing the Mo and Cu monitor foils were irradiated
with 18 MeV proton beams of 100 nA.
The activity was measured without chemical separation.
The energy loss was calculated by polynomial
approximation method of Andersen ad Ziegler.
The activities were determined from the photo-peak
areas Decay data the were taken from NUDAT of NNDC.
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the initial energy of the
bombarding beam was +- 0.3 MeV. The energy uncertainty
in the middle of each foil was estimated combining
the uncertainty of the energy degradation and energy
straggling.
The uncertainty of the energy varies between 0.3 MeV
for the first foil and increases up to 1.0 MeV at the
last foil in the stack.
The uncertainty of the cross sections
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters.
number of target atoms (5%)
detector efficiency (5%)
beam current (8%)
gamma intensities (1-5%)
statistical uncertainty (1-8%)
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes
TALYS(TENDL-2017) and EMPIRE3.2.2.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.

mentioned, but the used gamma lines are not 100%
